releaseNotes: v0.38.0 — Liquid medications now count in milliliters (mL), plus a faster unit-count workflow. RxRescue records the unit of measure for each medication (mL for liquids/suspensions, 'each' for tablets/capsules), filled in automatically from a Liberty stock import and shown on the quantity field ('Quantity (mL)'). A liquid bottle's count is its total mL (four 100 mL bottles = 400), so counts line up with Liberty. In a unit-count zone, scanning the same medication now rolls it into one running total instead of separate rows, and an edit popup appears after every scan with the quantity ready to type. The unit travels with catalog backups + CSV exports. Also folds in an internal design-system foundation (no visible change). The auto-updater rolls v0.36.0 -> v0.38.0 on next launch; the included database update is automatic and your data is unchanged. 772 tests passing; three-reviewer audit, no blocking findings.
